title: CBO Scaling Structure With Monthly Winner Rotation
insight: At $500K+/mo spend, use one CBO scaling campaign per product line with cost caps. Keep historical winners in one ad set, then add a new ad set each month containing that month's proven winners with a minimum spend limit. Turn off older ad sets as they lose steam. This prevents the campaign from getting top-heavy and spending everything on 1-2 ads, maintaining creative diversity within the scaling structure.
raw_excerpt: CBO scaling campaigns (one per product line): Cost cap on each CBO. Ad set 1: Historical winners that have been performing long term. New ad set added every month for that month's proven winners with a min spend limit. As older ad sets lose steam I turn them off. This keeps the campaign from getting top heavy and spending everything on 1-2 ads.
source_title: How I run FB ads for an 8 figure pet device company
actionable_steps: Create one CBO campaign per product line with cost caps
Maintain a permanent ad set for long-term historical winners
Add a new ad set monthly for that month's proven winners with minimum spend limits
Turn off older ad sets when performance declines to prevent top-heavy spend

title: Zombie Campaign Revives Failed Creatives at Low Cost Cap
insight: A 'zombie campaign' is a low cost cap CBO where every failed creative test gets dumped. Meta only spends when it finds a cheap conversion, so the campaign self-regulates. Surprisingly, many 'dead' ads come back to life at a low cost cap — the creative wasn't bad, it just couldn't compete at higher CPAs. This extracts residual value from creative investment that would otherwise be wasted.
raw_excerpt: Zombie campaign: Low cost cap CBO. Every failed test gets dumped in here. Meta only spends when it finds a cheap conversion. You'd be surprised how many 'dead' ads come back to life at a low cost cap.
source_title: How I run FB ads for an 8 figure pet device company
actionable_steps: Create a dedicated CBO campaign with a low cost cap as your 'zombie' campaign
Move all failed creative tests into this campaign instead of deleting them
Let Meta's algorithm find cheap conversions — don't manage this campaign actively
Monitor for revived performers that could be promoted back to scaling
